scores.name: Active voice
scores.suggestion: Rewrite passive constructions so the subject acts: 'Configure the setting' not 'The setting should be configured'.

scores.name: Second person
scores.evidence: 'you/your' appears 2 times; third-person references appear 1 times (67% second-person ratio).
scores.suggestion: Replace 'the user should' with 'you should'. Address the reader directly throughout.

scores.name: Imperative mood
scores.evidence: 2 of 4 list items start with an action verb (50%). Non-imperative example: "OAuth flow reference"
scores.suggestion: Start numbered steps and list items with an action verb: 'Click Save' not 'The Save button should be clicked'.

scores.name: Internal cross-links
scores.evidence: 3 internal link(s) found. Examples: "API Keys page", "OAuth flow reference", "Available scopes".
scores.suggestion: Add at least 3 links to related pages on the same domain to connect this page into the documentation graph.

scores.name: Related Topics section
scores.suggestion: Add a 'Related topics' or 'See also' section at the bottom of the page with links to adjacent content.

scores.name: Task-oriented link text
scores.evidence: All 3 links have descriptive anchor text.
scores.suggestion: Replace generic link text ('click here', bare URLs) with descriptive text that tells the reader what they'll find.

scores.name: Consistent capitalisation
scores.evidence: Dominant style is sentence case (1/3 headings). Inconsistent headings: "Option 1: API Key Authentication"; "See also".
scores.suggestion: Standardise heading capitalisation — pick either Title Case or Sentence case and apply it to all H2–H4 headings.

scores.name: Acronym expansion
scores.evidence: 1 acronym(s) used without expansion on first use: ADMIN.
scores.suggestion: Expand acronyms on first use: write 'Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)' before using RBAC alone.

scores.name: Descriptive page title
scores.evidence: Title is 35 characters: "Getting Started with Authentication".
scores.suggestion: Write a descriptive <title> of 30–65 characters that includes the primary topic keyword.

scores.name: Heading density
scores.evidence: 3 H2–H4 heading(s) for 53 words = 18 words per heading. Headings are too dense — sections are very short.
scores.suggestion: Aim for one H2–H4 heading per 150–400 words. Add headings to break up long sections or merge thin ones.

scores.name: Meta description present
scores.evidence: Meta description is 55 characters: "Learn how to authenticate using OAuth 2.0 and API keys.".
scores.suggestion: Add a <meta name='description'> tag with a 50–160 character summary of the page's purpose.

scores.name: Canonical URL
scores.suggestion: Add <link rel='canonical' href='...'> to the <head> to declare the preferred URL for this page.

scores.name: No third-person self-reference
scores.evidence: 1 third-person instruction(s) found. Example: "The user should".
scores.suggestion: Replace 'the user should configure' with 'you should configure'. Use second person for all instructions.

scores.name: No passive instructions
scores.suggestion: Rewrite passive instructions actively: 'Configure the timeout' not 'The timeout should be configured'.

scores.name: Direct address consistency
scores.evidence: Mostly second person style, but 1 instance(s) of the other style found (you/your: 2, third-person refs: 1).
scores.suggestion: Pick one address style and apply it throughout. Prefer second person ('you') for instructional content.
